evidence_quotes.quote: game distribution services such as Steam and ichi.io.

paragraphs.text: My current biggest concern related to this topic are payment processors which very recently went on broad spectrum campaing to affect game distribution services such as Steam and ichi.io. Payment processors such as Visa and Mastercard caused beformentioned sites to change their content based on criteria that were not tied to legality of contet as affected sites already had rules regard to illegal materials. Legal games were removed due to fact that payment processors didn't like their topic and that's it. This cause very dangerous precedents as its not only limitation of freedom of expression but also can be seen as market manipulation, because due to almost monolithic duopol of Visa and Mastercard they can freely choose which product can be sold and which can not. Payment processors shouldn't have this kind of power as they have no right to limit access to legal content just because they don't like it. If something is legal and is purchesed by adult person with their own money then this person should have full right to do so.
